steps.title: Compute the result
steps.content:
Take the square root, then add.
$$g(81)=9+300=309$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice C is correct. It's given that function $g$ is defined by $g(x)=\sqrt{x}+300$. Substituting $81$ for $x$ in function $g$ yields $g(81)=\sqrt{81}+300$, which is equivalent to $g(81)=9+300$, or $g(81)=309$. Therefore, the value of $g(x)$ when $x=81$ is $309$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is incorrect. This is the value of $\sqrt{81}$, not $\sqrt{81}+300$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice B is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice D is incorrect. This is the value of $81+300$, not $\sqrt{81}+300$.
